calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Oct-18-2018 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-09-2018 Set For Trial On Nov-05-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-29-2018 Continued To Mar-28-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-27-2017 Continued To Feb-14-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-11-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Corinna E. Meisner, Esq. of Hassard Bonnington LLP, Tel. 415.288.9800, for defendant and moving party;
Ross L. Libenson, Esq. of Libenson Law, Tel. 510.451.4441, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT MOHAMED ALI'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT as follows: Defendant Mohamed Ali's demurrer to the second and third causes of action in the complaint filed by plaintiff Michael Chatham is overruled as to the second cause of action for lack of informed consent and sustained without leave to amend as to the third cause of action for medical battery. While lack of informed consent is a species of negligence, it is a separate theory of liability with different elements than dental malpractice (compare CACI 400 and 500 with 533), and thus it is not inappropriate to allege it as a distinct claim. Mr. Chatham has not alleged, nor does it appear that he can allege, sufficient facts to support a medical battery claim because he gave permission to Dr. Ali to perform the type of dental work that Dr. Ali performed. That the dental work allegedly caused unconsented injury to Mr. Chatham's lip does not constitute medical battery. In addition to the tentative ruling, plaintiff's request for judicial notice is granted in part and denied in part. Defendant shall file an answer to the complaint by October 25, 2017.

Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Reyes Hunter CSR #6576 email: rhunter@sftc.org.
